title: Add 3 new test cases for direct-assign behavior (case-5..case-7)
description: Add three new test cases to tests/resolveCategory.test.js: (1) case-5-direct-assign-match — stub with directAssignment={assigned:true, category:'Groceries', mappingName:'Supermarkets', matchedKeyword:'interspar'}, transaction description 'INTERSPAR 2361 K4'; assert result.category='Groceries', autoRule='category_mapping_direct', classify call count=0; (2) case-6-direct-assign-miss — stub with directAssignment={assigned:false}, transaction 'RANDOM STORE'; assert classify called once, autoRule !== 'category_mapping_direct'; (3) case-7-mixed — two transactions in same test: first with keyword matching direct-assign mapping; second with different keyword matching only AI-hint mapping; assert both paths coexist without interference.
notes: All three tests use makeCategoryMappingStub helper from T-0066, plus existing stubs (makeAccountMappingStub, makeAutoCatStub, makePassthroughWordMapping). Case-7 mixed: simulate by creating two sequential app.resolveCategoryForTest() calls within the same test block, using two different stubs or the same stub with parameterized behavior. Pattern mirrors existing case-1..4 structure. Expected test run output: 21/21 (18 existing + 3 new).
verification_steps: case-5 passes: direct-assign match returns category directly, OpenAI not called, autoRule='category_mapping_direct'.
case-6 passes: direct-assign miss falls through to OpenAI, autoRule !== 'category_mapping_direct'.
case-7 passes: mixed scenario with direct-assign and AI-hint-only mappings coexist without interference.
All three tests use existing stubs + new makeCategoryMappingStub helper.
bash tests/run-tests.sh reports 21/21 passing (18 existing + 3 new).
No existing test modified — new tests are additions only.
